title: Battle of Serroco

{{Quote|I see what they're doing. I see a defense without honor. ''[…]'' Let them see what such a defense deserves. Let them burn.|Mandalore the Ultimate|<ref name="kotor14" />}}
The '''Battle of Serroco''', also called the '''Serroco tragedy''', the '''Onslaught on Serroco''', and the '''devastation of Serroco''', was an engagement during the [[Mandalorian Wars]] between the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders/Legends|Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders]] in the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[3963 BBY]]. Anticipating that the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorians]] would move on the muddy [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Serroco]] in the [[Expansion Region/Legends|Expansion Region]], [[Republic Navy/Legends|Republic Navy]] [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Saul Karath]] assembled a [[Battle Group Serroco|battle group]] and moved to defend the world. The Navy decide to place their camps next to Serroco's [[Stereb cities]], betting that the Mandalorians would not want to risk wrecking the [[City/Legends|cities]] and depriving themselves of the loot to be found within—they were wrong. When the Mandalorians arrived, their chief, [[Mandalore the Ultimate]], deemed the Republic tactic "without honor" and unleashed a barrage of [[Nuclear weapon/Legends|nuclear bombs]] that destroyed the Stereb cities. With Serroco devastated, Karath marshaled his remaining forces and retreated, hoping to link up with reinforcements further away from shattered Serroco. The Mandalorians pursued, finally catching up to Karath's [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]], the [[Courageous (Inexpugnable-class)|''Courageous'']], in deep [[Realspace/Legends|space]]. The ''Courageous'' was severely damaged and boarded, and Admiral Karath barely escaped with his life. The devastation of Serroco was decried as an atrocity and a crime by the Republic, and Serroco's memory became a rallying cry for the Republic throughout the rest of the war.

==Prelude==
{{Quote|It's a big moment for all of us, Carth. This time, we're ready for them!|Admiral Saul Karath, to Carth Onasi|<ref name="kotor15" />}}
[[File:Serroco_battle_group.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Battle Group Serroco]]
In [[3976 BBY]], the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] warrior clans, led by their chief [[Mandalore the Ultimate]], began conquering unaligned [[Planet/Legends|worlds]] on the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]]. After their crusades drew no action from the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], the Mandalorians began to lean towards the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core]], threatening resource worlds near the [[Ecumenopolis/Legends|ecumenopolis]] of [[Taris/Legends|Taris]]. The Republic finally responded, sending [[Republic Navy/Legends|Republic Navy]] forces out to the front lines to stand against the [[Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders/Legends|Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders]].<ref name="tea" /> True [[Mandalorian Wars|war]] finally broke out in [[3964 BBY]], with the Mandalorians launching a three-pronged offensive aimed at seizing Taris<ref name="kotor8">[[Knights of the Old Republic 8|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 8]]</ref>—the Mandalorians had little trouble conquering worlds like [[Vanquo]], and [[Taris Siege|besieged]] and occupied Taris. From there, the Mandalorians began to advance inwards. In [[3963 BBY]], the Mandalorians, moving from their stronghold of [[Jebble]], set their sights on the planet [[Serroco]], in the [[Expansion Region/Legends|Expansion Region's]]<ref name="tea" /> [[Ploo sector/Legends|Ploo sector]].<ref name="TEAOC">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}</ref> Serroco, a rainy, muddy world prone to violent storms, was the [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of the [[Stereb]] [[Species/Legends|species]]—and, by 3963 BBY, a full member of the Galactic Republic.<ref name="kotor14" /> The Republic saw the Mandalorian advance on Serroco coming, and [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Saul Karath]] of the Republic Navy assembled<ref name="kotor15" /> what he called [[Battle Group Serroco]]<ref name="karath" /> to defend the planet.<ref name="kotor15" />

The group was headed by the [[Inexpugnable-class tactical command ship|''Inexpugnable''-class tactical command ship]]<ref name="handbook">''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic Handbook]]''</ref> [[Courageous (Inexpugnable-class)|''Courageous'']], Karath's [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]]. The battle group set down camps near Serroco's [[Stereb cities]], which housed a great number of naval personnel alongside freelance tradespeople who tagged along, offering food and other services. Notably, Serroco's [[Camp Three (Serroco)|Camp Three]] featured the extremely popular ''[[Little Bivoli]]'' eatery. Unbeknownst to the Republic Navy, the ''Little Bivoli'' was run by two of the Republic's most wanted fugitives—[[Zayne Carrick]], a former [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] accused of [[Padawan Massacre|murdering]] his whole class on Taris, and his [[Snivvian/Legends|Snivvian]] "accomplice" [[Marn Hierogryph]]. By setting their camps so close to the cities, the Republic was hoping that they would be better able to protect them, counting on the idea that the Mandalorians would not want to destroy potentially valuable loot in the cities. In addition to the [[Republic Army]] troopers<ref name="kotor14" /> and Navy [[Ministry-class orbital shuttle|shuttles]] and other craft at the camps, Battle Group Serroco surrounded Serroco with a blockade of vessels, including [[Hammerhead-class cruiser|''Hammerhead''-class cruisers]], similarly-designed [[Hammerhead starship|starships]], [[Foray-class blockade runner|''Foray''-class blockade runners]] and a number of [[Aurek-class tactical strikefighter|''Aurek''-class strikefighters]].<ref name="kotor15" /> However, Carrick had a [[Force vision/Legends|vision]]—the former Jedi Padawan, while on a break from his duties at the ''Little Bivoli'', foresaw that [[Mandalore (title)/Legends|Mandalore]] the Ultimate would order the cities destroyed out of disgust for the Republic's tactics. Horrified, Carrick resolved to warn Admiral Karath and attempt to avert disaster.<ref name="kotor14" />

==The battle==
{{Dialogue|Morvis|Check that, check that. Bearing change on inbounds!|Karath|What?|Carrick|<s></s>''No!!!''|Morvis|Missiles tracking around ''Courageous''! Repeat, around ''Courageous''! They weren't aimed at the fleet! They're heading for—|attr=Commander Dallan Morvis, Admiral Saul Karath and Zayne Carrick before the missiles impact Serroco|src=<ref name="kotor15" />}}
Carrick hitched a ride up to the ''Courageous'' on Republic [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] [[Carth Onasi]]'s<ref name="kotor15" /> [[Conductor-class short-haul landing craft|short-haul craft]],<ref name="kotorcg" /> the ''[[Deadweight]]'', but upon reaching the [[Bridge (starship)/Legends|bridge]] was immediately recognized as a wanted fugitive. Karath thought that Carrick was a Mandalorian [[Spy/Legends|spy]], and refused to heed his warnings. Expecting the Mandalorians to arrive any [[Standard minute/Legends|minute]], Karath decided to keep Carrick on the bridge of the ''Courageous'', to show him what the Republic would do to Carrick's "friends"—sure enough, a Mandalorian battle group featuring a [[Kandosii-type dreadnaught|''Kandosii''-type dreadnaught]] and several [[Jehavey'ir-type assault ship|''Jehavey'ir''-type assault ships]] emerged from [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]]. It was small enough of a force to draw Karath's curiosity, as did their holding position at the edge of the Republic force's weapons perimeter. Without further ado, the Mandalorian ships launched a number of [[Nuclear weapon/Legends|nuclear bombs]] that appeared to be heading for the Republic [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]]. As Karath prepared countermeasures, the [[Missile/Legends|missiles]] suddenly changed course and angled around the ships—they were never aimed at the fleet. Just as Carrick had foreseen, the missiles hit the planet's surface, dealing heavy damage to Serroco's [[Human/Legends|Human]] settlements<ref name="kotor15" /> and turning the Stereb cities into glass craters.<ref name="kotorII">''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]''</ref>

[[File:BattleOfSerroco_dfna.jpg|thumb|right||250px|The ''Courageous'' battles [[Mandalorian warship (Venator-style)|Mandalorian warships]] in deep space]]
With the Republic force on Serroco decimated by the bombs destroying their camps—their missile-defense systems were not active, and thus only eight ships on the surface were able to make a getaway—Admiral Karath was left with no choice but to retreat and find a place to make a stand.<ref name="kotor15" /> With the ''Courageous'' group on the run, the Mandalorians pursued Karath and his forces through the [[Hyperspace route/Legends|lanes]] between Serroco and<ref name="serroco">{{KOTORbackups|16}}</ref> the [[Bank/Legends|banking]] world of<ref name="kotor11">[[Knights of the Old Republic 11|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 11]]</ref> [[Telerath/Legends|Telerath]]. Karath was hoping to make it to the planet [[Myrkr/Legends|Myrkr]] or the [[Ryyk Nebula/Legends|Ryyk Nebula]], where he could merge his forces with the ''[[Tremendous]]'' [[Tremendous group|group]] in the region, but faced major difficulties. Karath had sworn that he would not allow the Mandalorians to take any of the ships in his [[Flotilla/Legends|flotilla]] without a fight, and the ''Courageous'' saw plenty of action to this end.<ref name="serroco" /> The Mandalorians finally caught up to the ''Courageous'' in deep [[Realspace/Legends|space]], severely damaging the ''Courageous'' and boarding the listing vessel. Karath, Onasi, and [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Dallan Morvis]] were still alive, and made the decision that they had to scuttle the vessel. Faced with [[Neo-Crusader shock trooper]]s pouring into the ship, the three retreated from the bridge under heavy fire, with Onasi narrowly saving Admiral Karath from a direct attack.<ref name="kotor17" />

With Mandalorian troopers on three levels, Karath, Onasi and Morvis gathered several soldiers and made their way into the ship's brig, hoping to find a suitable place to make a stand. After barricading the door, they went straight for the [[Prison/Legends|prison]] cells, where the still-captive Zayne Carrick had been [[Meditation/Legends|meditating]]—as the Mandalorians drew nearer, Carrick rose from his spot to reveal that he had used [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] to gradually loosen bolts in the wall behind him, opening a passage from his cell to the ship's empty [[Hangar/Legends|cargo bay]]. Onasi was able to fly Karath, Morvis, Carrick and several troopers out of the fray in the ''Deadweight'', evading Mandalorian pursuit when the ship was noticed leaving the bay.<ref name="kotor17" /> With the loss of the ''Courageous'', the entirety of the Serroco battle group was lost.<ref name="kotor19" />

==Aftermath==
{{Quote|You were at Serroco, when they turned the Stereb cities into glass craters.|[[Atton Rand]], to Meetra Surik|<ref name="kotorII" />}}
[[File:Karathmeetmandalore.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Saul Karath and Mandalore the Ultimate face off, with the Mandalore holding his axe made of the remains of the ''Courageous'']]
After narrowly escaping [[Death/Legends|death]] on the ''Courageous'', Admiral Saul Karath and the passengers of the ''Deadweight'' were roped into a scheme by [[Arkoh Adasca]], head of the medical corporation [[The Adasca BioMechanical Corporation of Arkania|Adascorp]], to establish himself as a major [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] power by offering either the Republic or the Mandalorians the services of the massive and deadly creatures known as the [[Exogorth/Legends|exogorths]]. They came face-to-face with Mandalore the Ultimate himself in the process, and in the ensuing [[First Battle of Omonoth|chaos]] Adasca was killed, Carrick escaped custody, and Karath, Morvis and Onasi were able to slip away.<ref name="kotor21" /> Admiral Karath limped back to the Republic [[Capital/Legends|capital]] of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], and immediately submitted his resignation to the [[Unidentified Admiral of the Fleet|Admiral of the Fleet]], who refused to accept it and instead publicly praised Karath for his performance.<ref name="karath" /> Having claimed victory at Serroco, the Mandalorians moved Coreward to [[Nouane/Legends|Nouane]], another world that would end up being ravaged by the warrior clans.<ref name="egw">''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''</ref> Although Serroco was severely damaged as a result of the Mandalorian attack, Carth Onasi was able to save many Stereb lives by calling in warnings from the planet's [[Orbit/Legends|orbital]] [[Unidentified watchstation|watchstation]] and sending them to safety in Serroco's catacombs.<ref name="handbook" /> Life on Serroco did eventually continue, but irreparable damage had been done to the planet's surface<ref name="kotorII" />—furthermore, only eight of the ships grounded on the planet were able to make an escape.<ref name="kotor15" /> One of them carried [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Meetra Surik]], who was one of a number of Jedi regularly assigned to the world.<ref name="surik">In ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]'', the main character [[Meetra Surik]] is mentioned as having been on Serroco at the time of the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Battle of Serroco|attack]]. Since [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] involvement in the war had not yet begun, this was rectified by the later ''[[Galactic Republic Defense Ministry Daily Brief KD0092]]'' which says that there were Jedi on regular assignment to Serroco and one was able to get away on one of the few ships to escape. Logically, this would have to be Surik.</ref>

The loss of the ''Courageous'' threw some Republic planets, such as Telerath, into a panic,<ref name="kotor18">[[Knights of the Old Republic 18|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 18]]</ref> and the "crime" of Serroco's destruction was immediately condemned by the Republic.<ref name="invasion">{{KOTORbackups|18}}</ref> The ''Courageous'' itself was melted down for scrap by the Mandalorians, and part of it was fashioned into a [[Mandalore the Ultimate's battle-axe|battle-axe]] carried by Mandalore the Ultimate.<ref name="kotor20">[[Knights of the Old Republic 20|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 20]]</ref> The memory of Serroco would become a rallying cry for the Republic going forward in the war. In transmissions aimed at the Mandalorians, Republic propagandist [[Benegryph Goodvalor]] named his imaginary [[Warship/Legends|warship]] the ''Serroco'',<ref name="interference">{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/fiction/interference20090929.html|text="Interference"|int=Interference|newhost=Suvudu}}</ref> a name that would later be lent to a real Republic [[Serroco (starship)|ship]] by the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Galactic War]] several centuries later.<ref name="taral">{{TORcite|type=flashpoint|Taral V}}</ref> "Goodvalor" was a Serroco survivor himself—he was ''Little Bivoli'' host Marn Hierogryph, who, along with the [[Trandoshan/Legends|Trandoshan]] [[Chef/Legends|cook]] [[Slyssk]], had escaped the carnage in the nick of time by stealing a Republic troop [[Transport/Legends|transport]], saving half a sleeping [[Battalion/Legends|battalion]] in the process. For this, he was celebrated as the "Hero of Serroco", and the episode was turned into a propaganda story by the Republic [[Ministry of Defense|Defense Ministry]].<ref name="kotor47">[[Knights of the Old Republic 47|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 47]]</ref> About a decade after the Battle of Serroco, during the [[Dark Wars]],<ref name="kotorcg" /> a group of Republic veterans of the battle formed a [[Serroco (gang)|gang]] in the [[Refugee Sector]] of the Smuggler's Moon, [[Nar Shaddaa/Legends|Nar Shaddaa]].<ref name="kotorII" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Battle of Serroco was first mentioned in ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]'', a [[Star Wars video games|video game]] released in [[2004]]. The event was mentioned as part of the background of the game's main character, Meetra Surik<ref name="kotorII" />—it would not actually appear until [[Knights of the Old Republic 15|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 15]], a [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] written by [[John Jackson Miller]] and released in [[2007]]. The actual destruction of the planet takes place in ''Days of Fear''—<ref name="kotor15" />the conclusion of the space battle takes place in a later issue, [[Knights of the Old Republic 17|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 17]].<ref name="kotor17" />
